ALTERNATE-DAY FASTING PROTOCOL

Type O/A Alternate-Day Fasting Protocol

Structured alternate-day fasting with blood type-aligned nutrition for two

Paul's Core Goals: Target ~220lb goal weight and ~170g protein/day. Utilizing Type O-aligned choices including high-quality beef, chicken, salmon, and nutrient-dense vegetables.

Addie's Core Goals: Target ~130lb goal weight and ~129g protein/day. Utilizing Type A-aligned choices including salmon, tofu, beneficial fish, and vegetables.

FASTING SCHEDULE & PROTOCOL
ADF + Weekly Carb Cycle
SunLow-Carb
MonFast
TueLow-Carb
WedFast
ThuClean Carb
FriClean Carb
SatFast

The plan uses alternate-day fasting with 16/8 IF on all eating days. Eating days are not equal: Sunday and Tuesday are low-carb, while Thursday and Friday incorporate controlled clean-carb meals. Fasting days (Monday, Wednesday, Saturday) focus on electrolytes and hydration.

KEY PORTION ASSUMPTIONS
  • Beef (Type O): 10 lb purchased (8 lb eaten, 2 lb buffer). 1 lb 70/30 ground per dinner.
  • Chicken: 16 lb purchased. Paul 14 oz, Addie 10 oz per dinner.
  • Salmon (Mutually Beneficial): 10 lb box (8oz portions for dinners). On PWO meal days, 1 portion is split — 4oz per person each. 2 days/week post-workout. Perfect for both Type O and Type A.
  • Eggs: 72 eggs/month. 6 per workout day.
  • Cooking Fat: Moderate Organic Olive Oil/Beef Tallow/Ghee/Coconut Oil.
VEGETABLE PLAN SUMMARY

16 dinners per month structure emphasizing nutrient-dense options:

  • Broccoli: 8 lbs/mo (Blanch then sauté with garlic)
  • Zucchini: 4 lbs/mo (Sautéed quarter moons)
  • Leeks: 2 lb batch covers 2 dinners (Cheese sauce/roux)
  • Kabocha Squash: 5 lb squash covers 2 dinners
  • Spinach: 2 lbs/month (primarily for post-workout eggs)

On Sale Rotations: Mix things up with Green Beans, Asparagus, Swiss Chard, Spaghetti Squash, Romaine, or Chayote when discounted.

TYPE-O: LOW-CARB DINNER IDEAS
  • Burger Bowls: Served over sautéed zucchini or broccoli.
  • Garlic Herb Beef: Ground beef sautéed with garlic, herbs, and green beans.
  • Cheesy Beef Patties: Beef patties paired with broccoli and a creamy leek cheese sauce.
  • Zucchini Beef Skillet: Ground beef cooked in a skillet with zucchini quarter moons.
  • Beef & Kabocha: Ground beef and kabocha squash plate, perfect for higher-carb days.
TYPE-A: EASY TOFU DINNER IDEAS

Tip: Use Extra-Firm for steaks & crispy frying, Firm for stir-fries, and Silken for blending into sauces.

  • Crispy Honey-Soy (Extra-Firm): Press, cube, toss in soy/garlic/ginger, pan-sear, glaze.
  • Broccoli Stir-Fry (Firm): Pan-fry cubes, toss with blanched broccoli & savory brown sauce.
  • Spicy Garlic Tofu (Firm): Seared tofu in a chili-garlic sauce over sautéed greens.
  • Baked Lemon-Herb (Extra-Firm): Marinate in lemon/olive oil/herbs, bake until firm.
  • Hearty Miso Soup (Silken): Cubed silken tofu simmered in a rich miso broth with spinach, leeks, and mushrooms.
DAILY FASTING SCHEDULE
8:00 PM Start Fasting
Morning Electrolytes & Water Only
12:00 PM Break Fast
12PM - 8PM Eating Window (8 Hours)

POST-WORKOUT MEAL & SHAKE
  • Tues/Thurs PWO Meal: 2-4 eggs + 4oz wild-caught salmon. Sear salmon in tallow or clarified butter/ghee; eggs any style.
  • Sun PWO Meal: 2-4 eggs + canned mackerel (replaces salmon for variety).
  • Protein Shake (all PWO days, 32 oz batch):
    • Split 16 oz Paul / 16 oz Addie
    • Plant Protein: 2 scoops (34g) + Hydrolyzed Collagen: 2 scoops (20g) + Chicken Bone Broth: 1 scoop (10g)
    • 10 oz soy milk + 8 oz water to start
    • 1 cup frozen fruit blend - pineapple, cherry, blueberry
    • 1 tsp agave
    • Blend, then add water only until desired consistency
ELECTROLYTES & FASTING
  • Daily 16/8 Protocol: Critical for hydration on Mon, Wed, Sat.
  • Quarterly Extended Fast: 4.5 days every 12 weeks. Starts Tuesday evening, ends Sunday afternoon. Break fast with bone broth only.
  • Key Benefits of Fasting:
    • Autophagy: Cellular cleanup process that clears out damaged cells & toxins.
    • Stem Cell Creation: Deep fasting triggers immune system regeneration and new stem cell production.
    • Other Benefits: Insulin sensitivity reset, reduced inflammation, and mental clarity.
  • Electrolyte Recipe (per 32 oz water):
    • 1/4 tsp gray Celtic sea salt, 1/8 tsp potassium salt
    • 1/2 tsp True Lemon/Lime powder, 1/4 tsp Magnesium citrate powder
MONTHLY SHOPPING ROUTINE
  • Restaurant Depot: 10 lb box Salmon (8oz portions)
  • Stater Bros: 10 lb Beef brisket for custom grinding. Get free suet.
  • Costco: 16 lb Air-chilled chicken breast (2 x 8lb packs)
  • Sprouts: 3 packs of 24 Pasture-raised organic eggs
  • Weekly Produce (Mostly H-Mart): Broccoli, Zucchini, Leeks, Kabocha, Spinach.
CORE COOKING FAT SYSTEM
  • Single-Source Organic Olive Oil: Low/medium heat. Ideal for dressings, finishing, and light sauteing.
  • Beef Tallow: High heat. Excellent for searing steaks and roasting root vegetables.
  • Clarified Butter/Ghee: Medium/high heat. Great for eggs, curries, and general cooking with a buttery flavor.
  • Coconut Oil: Medium heat. Best for baking, curries, and tropical flavor profiles.

FINALIZED SOURCING DETAILS
  • Soy Milk: 4 quarts of soy milk per month required for shakes.
  • Spinach: 1 lb every 2 weeks (2 lbs total per month).
  • Beef Sourcing: Brisket from Stater Bros at $7.99/lb. They are flexible and cut to order.
  • Cheese (Leek Dinners): 5 lb block of Mozzarella from Restaurant Depot for $12.
MONTHLY BUDGET TOTALS
  • Grass-Fed Beef: $80 (10 lb @ $8/lb)
  • Air-Chilled Chicken: $60 (16 lb @ $3.75/lb)
  • Wild-Caught Salmon: $80 (10 lb box, 20 portions @ 8oz)
  • Organic Pasture-Raised Eggs: $40 (3 x 24-packs @ ~$13.33/each)
  • Organic Vegetables: ~$90-100/month
  • Core Food Total: ~$350-360/month
  • Misc. (fruit, soy milk, pantry): ~$30-50/month
  • Total Budget: ~$380-410 / month
FREEZER & PREP SYSTEM
  • Chicken: Vacuum pack in two-breast packs. Freeze flat.
  • Beef: Grind monthly. Mix suet for 70/30. Freeze in 1 lb flattened packs inside gallon bags.
  • Salmon: Keep 8oz portions frozen individually.
  • Eggs: Buy 2 packs bi-weekly for optimal freshness.
WEEKLY CARB CYCLE STRATEGY

An ADF-supported weekly carb cycle: low-carb depletion days early in the week, followed by controlled clean-carb refill days for recovery, adherence, and performance. Eating days are intentionally structured — not interchangeable.

▼ DEPLETE PHASE — Low-Carb + Fasting
▼ REFILL PHASE
▼ RESET
SUN
Low-Carb
Protein-first + healthy fats
MON
Fast
Electrolytes + hydration
TUE
Low-Carb
Protein-first + depletion
WED
Fast
Electrolytes + hydration
THU
Clean-Carb Refill
Primary refill · 100–175g
FRI
Controlled Carb
Dinner-focused · 75–150g
SAT
Fast
Electrolytes + hydration
Low-Carb Eating Days
Sunday + Tuesday
  • Protein-first meals
  • Higher fat: olive oil, tallow, ghee
  • Carbs from veg + small fruit/shake
  • Avoid stacking large carbs with heavy fats
Fasting Days
Monday + Wednesday + Saturday
  • Electrolytes + hydration
  • Keep protocol simple and consistent
  • Do not compensate with oversized meals the next day
Clean-Carb Days
Thursday + Friday
  • Protein first, always
  • Thursday is the primary refill day
  • Friday stays controlled and dinner-focused
  • Use carbs intentionally — not as a reward meal
Carb Targets
  • Thursday: 100–175g carbs
  • Friday: 75–150g carbs
  • If fat loss stalls 2+ weeks, reduce Friday carbs first before touching Thursday.
Best Clean Carb Choices
Sweet Potato White Rice Kabocha Quinoa Lentils Brown Rice Fruit / Berries Mango Oats (if tolerated) Sourdough (occasionally)
Low-Carb Day Anchors
  • Proteins: Beef, chicken, salmon, eggs, tofu/fish where appropriate.
  • Vegetables: Broccoli, zucchini, spinach, leeks, green beans, asparagus, chard.
  • Fats: Olive oil, beef tallow, clarified butter/ghee, coconut oil.
Practical Bottom Line

Low-carb Sun/Tue → Fast Mon/Wed → Clean carbs Thu/Fri → Fast Sat. More sustainable than a rigid approach while still keeping fat-loss pressure high through fasting, protein control, and planned carb timing. Judge progress by weekly trend — not next-day scale weight.

PRACTICAL REFERENCE ADD-ONS

Low-Carb Dessert Options

Almond Flour Brownies Almond flour brownies using almond flour, cocoa, eggs, and monk fruit.
Walnut Bark Dark chocolate walnut bark — melted dark chocolate poured over walnuts and chilled.
Silken Tofu Mousse Blended silken tofu with cocoa powder and monk fruit. Protein-rich, Type A–friendly.
Coconut Chia Pudding Chia seeds soaked in coconut milk overnight with a few drops of vanilla and monk fruit.

Rules & Guides

  • Calorie Control: Adjust cooking fats and cheese portions first when cutting calories.
  • Sauce/Flavor: Leverage garlic, ginger, soy, and herbs to elevate basics without sugar.
  • Rotation Strategy: Rotate primary proteins weekly to prevent fatigue.
  • Macro Knobs: Low-carb on Sun/Tue; clean carbs (sweet potato, white rice, kabocha) on Thu/Fri. Reduce Friday carbs first if fat loss stalls. Keep Thursday as the primary recovery/refill day.
  • Prep Checklist: Check freezer inventory monthly; restock bulk proteins on sale.
